name: THINGS-data (fMRI, MEG & Behavior)
description: A multimodal collection probing how the brain represents objects and concepts: dense 7T fMRI from 3 participants and MEG from 4 participants viewing thousands of images spanning 1,854 object concepts, plus a 12,340-participant behavioral similarity dataset. Many concepts are people, faces, and socially meaningful objects, making THINGS useful for situating face and social perception within a broad, well-characterized semantic space.
citation: Hebart MN, Contier O, Teichmann L, et al. (2023). THINGS-data, a multimodal collection of large-scale datasets for investigating object representations in human brain and behavior. eLife, 12, e82580.
